context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements SFCC import automation using WebDAV for file upload and the Jobs API for job triggering. Focuses on correct URL patterns, authentication methods, job parallelism, parameter names, and diagnostic logging.

checklist.name: WebDAV URL pattern
checklist.description: File upload uses the WebDAV URL pattern: {instanceUrl}/on/demandware.servlet/webdav/Sites/Impex/src/{filename}

checklist.name: WebDAV Basic auth
checklist.description: WebDAV PUT requests use Basic authentication with clientId:clientSecret base64-encoded (not Bearer token)

checklist.name: WebDAV PUT method
checklist.description: File uploads use HTTP PUT method (not POST or multipart)

checklist.name: Jobs API Bearer auth
checklist.description: Job trigger requests use Bearer token authentication (not Basic auth)

checklist.name: ImportFile parameter
checklist.description: Job trigger request body includes a parameter named exactly 'ImportFile' with the filename as its value

checklist.name: Separate job IDs
checklist.description: Catalog, inventory, and pricebook imports use separate/distinct job IDs (not one shared job definition for all three)

checklist.name: Parallel execution
checklist.description: Catalog and inventory job triggers are initiated concurrently (e.g. using Promise.all or similar parallel execution pattern)

checklist.name: Zero-records logging
checklist.description: import-log-schema.md or script comments mention checking for '0 records processed' and the likely cause (file path mismatch or ImportFile parameter mismatch)

checklist.name: Log output present
checklist.description: Script includes console.log or similar logging after each upload and job trigger step (not just on error)

checklist.name: catalogID parameter
checklist.description: Catalog import job trigger body includes a parameter named 'catalogID' with the catalog ID value
